frameworks.name: successor representation
frameworks.one_line: value decomposed into a successor map (expected future state occupancy) times a reward predictor

frameworks.name: deep reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: end-to-end neural-network realization of successor representations from raw pixels

frameworks.name: model-free reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: value-function learning without explicit world-dynamics factorization

frameworks.name: model-based reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: value-function learning via an explicit transition model

core_question: Can the successor representation be generalized within an end-to-end deep reinforcement learning framework that operates on raw observations?
key_assumptions: value can be factored as the inner product of a successor map and a reward weight vector
successor map represents expected future state occupancy from any given state
raw pixel observations suffice as the input state representation
manipulations: environment type (MazeBase grid-worlds vs. Doom game engine)
policy used to train successor maps (including random policy for subgoal extraction)
key_findings: DSR learns value functions end-to-end from raw pixels in grid-world and Doom environments
factorizing reward and world dynamics yields increased sensitivity to distal reward changes
bottleneck states (subgoals) can be extracted from successor maps trained under a random policy
interpretations: decomposing value into successor map plus reward predictor is a viable third path between model-free and model-based deep RL
successor maps provide a representation from which structural features like subgoals naturally emerge
